creator: Tim Fernholz
title: (No AI) The “Father of the Internet” is finally retiring
dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
content: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.
contentSnippet: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

title: (No AI) Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_C_96d15c.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In [a post on X](https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2072106151890809341?), Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access tomorrow to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline.

[Anthropic](https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2072106151890809341?) wrote:

> We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
> 
> We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
> 
> We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/958964/anthropic-claude-fable-5-is-back)
contentSnippet:
After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access tomorrow to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline. 
Anthropic wrote:
We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

Read the full story at The Verge.

In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access tomorrow to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft […]

title: (No AI) Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Screenshots of NotebookLM’s shorts feature](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/notebooklm-shorts.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate 60-second vertical AI clips based on the sources you upload to the app.

The [example shared by Google](https://x.com/NotebookLM/status/2071987494799716626) details Australia's unsuccessful war on emus, pairing paper cutout-style AI art of emus with narration. It adds to some of the other ways NotebookLM lets you interact with your research, including by generating [AI podcasts](https://www.theverge.com/2024/12/13/24318099/google-notebooklm-audio-overviews-talk-plus), [cinematic videos](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/889475/notebooklm-can-now-summarize-research-in-cinematic-video-overviews), and visual explainers.

> Doom scrolling but make it educational ðŸ¤“  
>   
> Introducing Short Video Overviews in Notebo …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/959778/google-notebooklm-ai-clips)

The example shared by Google details Australia's unsuccessful war on emus, pairing paper cutout-style AI art of emus with narration. It adds to some of the other ways NotebookLM lets you interact with your research, including by generating AI podcasts, cinematic videos, and visual explainers.

title: (No AI) The Trump Administration Is Lifting Its Export Controls on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable AI Models
dc:creator: Hugo Lowell
content: The White House is easing restrictions on Anthropic’s most advanced AI models weeks after ordering the company to suspend access for foreign nationals.

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Bernie Sanders Saw This Coming
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: For decades, the senator has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping point.
contentSnippet: For decades, the senator has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping point.
